paper_title: Oilseed Rape Growth and Nitrogen Use Efficiency Study Methods

methods.description: Describes the design and establishment of field and pot experiments, including site selection, soil characterization, genotype selection based on nitrogen response, application of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium fertilizers, and the experimental layout using a randomized complete block design.

steps.description: In this study, a field and a pot experiments were conducted at Yangling district, Shaanxi province, P.R. China (34°24 0 N, 108°08 0 E) from 2016-2018.
steps.searchable_summary: Establish field and pot experiments at a specific geographical location over a defined period.
inputs: Location (Yangling district, Shaanxi province, P.R. China)

steps.description: In the field trail, the soil was with a pH of 7.65, containing 13.7 g kg -1 organic matter, 1.19 g of total N kg -1 , 24.7 mg of available N kg -1 , 15.7 mg of Olsen-P kg -1 and 76.9 mg of available K kg -1 .
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize soil properties for the field experiment site.
outputs: Field soil properties (pH, organic matter, total N, available N, Olsen-P, available K)

steps.description: In the pot experiment, the soil had the following properties: 9.21 g kg -1 organic matter, 0.92 g of total N kg -1 , 22.0 mg of available N kg -1 , 10.7 mg of Olsen-P kg -1 and 73.6 mg of available K kg -1 , and a pH of 7.5.
steps.searchable_summary: Characterize soil properties for the pot experiment soil.
outputs: Pot soil properties (pH, organic matter, total N, available N, Olsen-P, available K)

steps.description: Our previous study indicated that the Nt-responder and Nt-nonresponder genotypes were expressed only under high N supply conditions, while the Nt-efficient and Nt-inefficient were expressed under low N supply conditions. Therefore, the 10 high N genotypes were planted with high N level (150 kg N ha -1 in the field experiment and 0.30 g of N kg -1 dry soil in the pot experiment), while the 8 low N genotypes were planted with low N level (0 kg N ha -1 in field experiment and 0.10 g of N kg -1 dry soil in pot experiment).
steps.searchable_summary: Select and plant genotypes based on their nitrogen response and apply corresponding high or low nitrogen fertilizer levels.
inputs: Genotype N response classification (from previous study)
High N level (field: 150 kg N ha-1)
High N level (pot: 0.30 g N kg-1 dry soil)
Low N level (field: 0 kg N ha-1)
Low N level (pot: 0.10 g N kg-1 dry soil)
outputs: Planted genotypes under specific N treatments in field and pot experiments

steps.description: In the field experiment, the high or low plot of 10 m in length 9 2 m wide or 8 m length 9 2 m width, consisted of forty or thirty-two rows of oilseed rape with row spacing of 50 cm.
steps.searchable_summary: Design and layout field experiment plots including dimensions, number of rows, and row spacing.
inputs: Number of genotypes (high N: 10, low N: 8)
outputs: Field plot layout (dimensions, rows per plot, spacing)

steps.description: Each experiment was arranged in a randomized complete block design with four replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement a randomized complete block experimental design with four replicates for both field and pot experiments.
inputs: List of treatments (genotype x N level combinations)

steps.description: In both experiments, sufficient phosphorus as superphosphate (P 2 O 5 135 kg ha -1 and 0.20 g kg -1 dry soil) and potassium (K 2 O 150 kg ha -1 and 0.30 g kg -1 dry soil) were supplied.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply phosphorus and potassium fertilizers at specified rates to both field and pot experiments.
inputs: P application rate (field: 135 kg P2O5 ha-1)
P application rate (pot: 0.20 g P2O5 kg-1 dry soil)
K application rate (field: 150 kg K2O ha-1)
K application rate (pot: 0.30 g K2O kg-1 dry soil)

methods.description: Procedure for monitoring the developmental stages of oilseed rape siliques and measuring various physiological and morphological parameters on selected siliques in the pot experiment.

steps.description: In order to monitor the dynamics of silique development, flowering and fertilization process of the genotypes were categorized into: pre-embryo (1-4 days after flowering), globular (5-8 days after flowering), heart (9-14 days after flowering), torpedo (15-22 days after flowering), and maturation (23-30 days after flowering) stages (Andriotis et al. 2010;Tan et al. 2011;Hehenberger et al. 2012).
steps.searchable_summary: Define and categorize silique developmental stages based on days after flowering.

steps.description: For accurate monitoring, fully-opened flowers on the main inflorescence of each plant were marked with colored strings each day.
steps.searchable_summary: Mark newly opened flowers on the main inflorescence of each plant daily.
outputs: Marked flowers with known flowering date

steps.description: In the pot experiment, five marked siliques of each plant at each developmental stage were chosen for the determination of net photosynthetic rate (lmol CO 2 Ám -2 Ás -1 ), silique length (cm), width (cm), surface area (cm 2 ), EON, biomass (mg) and RNA expression levels.
steps.searchable_summary: Select a sample of marked siliques at each developmental stage from each plant in the pot experiment for subsequent measurements.
inputs: Marked siliques with known developmental stage

steps.description: Determine net photosynthetic rate (lmol CO 2 Ám -2 Ás -1 ) of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the net photosynthetic rate of selected siliques.
outputs: Net photosynthetic rate data (lmol CO2 m-2 s-1)

steps.description: Measure silique length (cm) of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the length of selected siliques.

steps.description: Measure silique width (cm) of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the width of selected siliques.

steps.description: Measure silique surface area (cm 2 ) of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the surface area of selected siliques.

steps.description: Determine EON of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the EON (Embryo Ovule Number) of selected siliques.

steps.description: Measure biomass (mg) of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure the biomass (dry weight) of selected siliques.

steps.description: Determine RNA expression levels of chosen siliques.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine RNA expression levels in selected silique samples.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring stamen number, anther number, pollen grain number, and pollen viability from sampled flower buds.

steps.description: At the flowering stage, we removed the petals from the sampled buds with tweezers.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove petals from sampled flower buds using tweezers.

steps.description: and recorded the stamen and anther number.
steps.searchable_summary: Count and record the number of stamens and anthers in the dissected flower buds.

steps.description: Then, the pollen grain number and pollen viability (%) were measured according to Lankinen et al. (2018): Pollen grains were sprinkled on a microscope slide in Hoekstra medium with 16% sucrose.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare microscope slides with pollen grains in Hoekstra medium for viability testing.

steps.description: The microscope slides were placed in a dark constant temperature incubator with a temperature of 25C for 3 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate pollen slides in a dark, temperature-controlled environment (25C) for 3 hours.

steps.description: Pollen germination was terminated by adding 100% glycerol.
steps.searchable_summary: Stop pollen germination by adding glycerol to the slides.

steps.description: Pollen grain number and germination rate (pollen viability) (%) were determined under a light microscope (Axioplan 2; Zeiss) as the percentage of germinated pollen grains from 100 pollen grains in a randomly chosen area.
steps.searchable_summary: Count total and germinated pollen grains under a light microscope in a random area.

steps.description: with the following equation: Pollen viability (%) = the total number of germinated pollen grains/the total number of pollen grains 9 100%.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate pollen viability percentage using the counts of germinated and total pollen grains.

methods.description: Procedure for determining the initial number of ovules and the rate of ovule abortion.

steps.description: The initiation ovule number and abortion rate were determined according to Wang et al. (2011): Determine the initial ovule number.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the initial number of ovules per silique.

steps.description: Determine the aborted ovule number.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the number of aborted ovules per silique.

steps.description: Ovule abortion rate (%) = the aborted ovule number/the initial ovule number.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the ovule abortion rate percentage using the counts of aborted and initial ovules.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring key yield components including the number of siliques per plant, 1000-seed weight, and seed yield per plant.

steps.description: Number of siliques per plant was measured as the number of effective pods on the main inflorescence, branch inflorescence and the whole plant (Shi et al. 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: Count the number of effective siliques on the main inflorescence, branch inflorescence, and whole plant.

steps.description: Seed weight (g) of each plant was measured by weighing 500 fully developed seeds with four replications.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh 500 fully developed seeds from each plant with four replicate weighings.
inputs: Fully developed seeds from each plant

steps.description: the weight of 500 seeds was then converted to 1,000-seed weight for easy comparison with other studies (Fan et al. 2010).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert the weight of 500 seeds to 1000-seed weight.

steps.description: Seed yield per plant (g) was measured as the average dry weight of seeds of the four randomly selected plants from each genotype (Shi et al. 2011).
steps.searchable_summary: Select four random plants from each genotype and measure the average dry weight of their seeds to determine seed yield per plant.
inputs: Drying oven (if dry weight needed, implied)

methods.description: Procedure for determining the nitrogen concentration in seed and straw samples using the Kjeldahl method and calculating nitrogen accumulation in seeds and shoots.

steps.description: Each of the ground seed and straw samples was weighed.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh ground seed and straw samples.

steps.description: and then digested and determined for N concentration by following the Kjeldahl procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest ground seed and straw samples and determine nitrogen concentration using the Kjeldahl procedure.

steps.description: Seed and shoot (seed ? straw) N accumulations (g) were calculated as the product of dry matter by the respective N concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate seed nitrogen accumulation by multiplying seed dry matter (weight) by seed nitrogen concentration.

steps.description: Calculate straw nitrogen accumulation by multiplying straw dry matter (weight) by straw nitrogen concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate straw nitrogen accumulation by multiplying straw dry matter (weight) by straw nitrogen concentration.

steps.description: Calculate shoot nitrogen accumulation as the sum of seed N accumulation and straw N accumulation.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate shoot nitrogen accumulation as the sum of seed and straw nitrogen accumulation.

methods.description: Calculation of Nitrogen Utilization Efficiency (NUtE) and Nitrogen Uptake Efficiency (NUpE) based on seed yield, shoot nitrogen accumulation, and nitrogen supply.

steps.description: According to He et al. (2017), N utilization efficiency (NUtE) (g / g) and N uptake efficiency (NUpE) (g / g) were estimated with the following equations: NUtE (g / g) = seed yield / shoot N accumulation;
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Nitrogen Utilization Efficiency (NUtE) using the formula: seed yield / shoot N accumulation.

steps.description: NUpE (g / g) = shoot N accumulation / N supply.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Nitrogen Uptake Efficiency (NUpE) using the formula: shoot N accumulation / N supply.
